title: Elementary process plant occupations n.e.c.
description: Perform a variety of attending duties at amusement or recreation facility. May schedule use of recreation facilities, maintain and provide equipment to participants of sporting events or recreational pursuits, or operate amusement concessions and rides. Directly supervise and coordinate activities of construction or extraction workers. Direct work crews installing residential or commercial solar photovoltaic or thermal systems. (Based on 74 related US occupations.)

work_activities.element_name: Communicating with Supervisors, Peers, or Subordinates
Operating Vehicles, Mechanized Devices, or Equipment
Evaluating Information to Determine Compliance with Standards
Judging the Qualities of Objects, Services, or People
Estimating the Quantifiable Characteristics of Products, Events, or Information
Coordinating the Work and Activities of Others
Interpreting the Meaning of Information for Others
Resolving Conflicts and Negotiating with Others
Providing Consultation and Advice to Others
Communicating with People Outside the Organization
Drafting, Laying Out, and Specifying Technical Devices, Parts, and Equipment
Performing for or Working Directly with the Public

work_context.element_name: Face-to-Face Discussions with Individuals and Within Teams
Work With or Contribute to a Work Group or Team
Deal With External Customers or the Public in General
Coordinate or Lead Others in Accomplishing Work Activities
Health and Safety of Other Workers
Work Outcomes and Results of Other Workers
Dealing With Unpleasant, Angry, or Discourteous People
Dealing with Violent or Physically Aggressive People
Outdoors, Exposed to All Weather Conditions
In an Open Vehicle or Operating Equipment
In an Enclosed Vehicle or Operate Enclosed Equipment
Exposed to Sounds, Noise Levels that are Distracting or Uncomfortable
Exposed to Very Hot or Cold Temperatures
Exposed to Extremely Bright or Inadequate Lighting Conditions
Exposed to Cramped Work Space, Awkward Positions
Exposed to Minor Burns, Cuts, Bites, or Stings
Spend Time Climbing Ladders, Scaffolds, or Poles
Spend Time Kneeling, Crouching, Stooping, or Crawling
Spend Time Keeping or Regaining Balance
Spend Time Using Your Hands to Handle, Control, or Feel Objects, Tools, or Controls
Spend Time Bending or Twisting Your Body
Wear Common Protective or Safety Equipment such as Safety Shoes, Glasses, Gloves, Hearing Protection, Hard Hats, or Life Jackets
Wear Specialized Protective or Safety Equipment such as Breathing Apparatus, Safety Harness, Full Protection Suits, or Radiation Protection
Impact of Decisions on Co-workers or Company Results
Importance of Being Exact or Accurate
Pace Determined by Speed of Equipment

tasks.task: Add chemicals, such as ammonia, chlorine, or lime, to disinfect and deodorize water and other liquids.
Help coordinate activities of construction or extraction workers, including providing assistance to craft workers such as earth drillers and derrick operators.
Unload materials, devices, and machine parts, and organize materials to prepare for use, including transporting tools or materials to work sites using hand tools and vehicles.
Clean up work areas and remove debris after activities are complete, and maintain equipment and work environments to ensure safety and cleanliness.
Drive trucks through designated routes and confirm routes and destination information for transportation of materials, while ensuring safe operating conditions and reporting issues.
Monitor and inspect equipment operation and safety conditions, ensuring tanks, meters, and gauges are functioning correctly and reporting abnormalities as necessary.
Set up and adjust equipment, including driving and operating vehicles and machinery, completing load, unload, and identification of materials, and mixing or pumping materials.
Collect and test material samples and maintain operational data for reporting purposes, including record keeping and compliance with procedures.
Perform excavation and construction tasks, including digging trenches, cutting materials, and securing fixtures while adhering to safety processes.
Maintain and repair machinery, equipment, or tools, including lubrication and regular maintenance to keep operational effectiveness.
Assist in the installation of structures, such as scaffolding or piping, ensuring proper support and alignment as required for construction operations.
Supervise and coordinate workers engaged in maintenance and operational activities to ensure efficient workplace operations.
Prepare surfaces and materials for various applications, such as cleaning, applying substances, or ensuring quality finishes are met according to specifications.
Operate and maintain auxiliary equipment and monitor safety conditions in work environments to meet operational standards.

Measure, cut, thread, and assemble new pipes; install copper-clad ground rods using a manual post driver; mount brackets and hangers on walls and ceilings for pipe support; requisition tools and equipment, select type and size of pipe, and transport materials and equipment to work site; fit valves, couplings, or assemblies to tanks, pumps, or systems with hand tools; assist pipe fitters in laying out, assembling, and installing piping for various systems; excavate, grade ditches, and lay and join pipe for water and sewer services; clean work areas and machines using solvents; maintain tools, vehicles, and equipment; and document work activities and maintain reports.

Disassemble and remove damaged or worn pipe; clean, sterilize, and maintain equipment, machinery, and work stations; load and unload materials and tools from work trucks; observe equipment operations to detect malfunctions; and perform emergency repairs and general maintenance for a variety of systems.
Perform ventilation tasks and conduct safety checks on equipment before operating; hoist roofing materials using ropes or ladders; and attach roofing materials such as tiles and shingles using nails.

Maintain processing, production, and inspection information and reports; monitor operation and adjust controls of processing machines and equipment; and inspect materials and components for defects using measuring instruments.
Perform various drilling and excavation activities to locate and assess geological formations; drive trucks or tractors to and from work sites; and retrieve lost equipment from drilling locations.

Set, adjust, and readjust computerized or mechanical equipment controls to regulate processing parameters; manipulate valves, switches, and buttons to control machine operations; and document and prepare reports of work activities.
Guide and assist with installation tasks such as flushing, sterilizing, and decontaminating wells; and perform tests to assess well performance or inspect core samples for analysis.
Perform tasks related to rock excavation, including drilling holes for bolts, determining locations for cuts, and splitting rock with hydraulic mechanisms.
Observe packages on conveyors for quality control; and load and adjust materials or products on conveyors by hand or using equipment.
Excavate, grade, and lay pipe for water and sewer services, and perform site activities required of green certified construction practices, such as waste management procedures.
Perform safety checks on drilling equipment, and make adjustments as necessary based on monitoring conditions.
Transport equipment, tools, and materials to work sites using diverse methods including motorized trucks and hand tools.
Conduct methane gas checks to ensure quality of air in breathing spaces; and maintain ventilation systems in confined work areas.
Drive machines into position for drilling; and operate machines for materials processing in mining or excavation.
Maintain tools and equipment; and clear work areas of debris and unused materials to ensure safety and efficiency.
Monitor drilling operations to adjust techniques based on ongoing assessments; and manage the flow of materials during excavation activities.
Perform rough-ins for installations and repairs and locate, repair, or replace defective systems.
Assist in the layout and construction of various structures and materials, ensuring compliance with design specifications for repairs and updates.
Monitor gauges, recording instruments, flowmeters, or products to ensure that specified conditions are maintained, and observe safety precautions to prevent fires or explosions.
Perform routine maintenance and upgrade work on machines and equipment, including lubricating, cleaning, and inspecting machines for hazards, operating efficiency, malfunctions, wear, or leaks.
Record operational data, such as temperatures, pressures, ingredients used, and processing times, and maintain production records such as quantities and dimensions of materials processed.
Communicate processing instructions to other workers, and direct activities of workers assisting in control or verification of processes or in unloading materials.
Select, measure, weigh, and mix chemical or raw materials according to specifications and load them into tanks, vats, or other equipment for processing.
Operate and control machinery and equipment in which chemical changes or reactions occur during processing, ensuring optimal performance by adjusting controls as necessary.
Examine and measure completed materials or products to verify conformance to specifications, using measuring devices such as tape measures, gauges, or calipers.
Assemble equipment and attach pipes, fittings, or valves, and perform minor repairs and maintenance to processing equipment as needed.
Drive machines into piles of material blasted from working faces to facilitate processing or movement of materials.
Direct workers to move stakes, place blocks, position anchors or cables, or move materials as needed.
Clean, fuel, service, and perform safety checks on equipment; inspect and repair machinery and equipment as needed, ensuring proper operation and maintenance.
Maintain logs of instrument readings, test results, or shift production for entry in computer databases, and record production data and material movements.
Operate machinery and equipment related to construction and maintenance tasks, including moving materials, grading, and excavation.
Monitor operations to ensure safety and compliance with applicable regulations; observe and record equipment operations to detect malfunctions and notify operators as necessary.
Examine products to verify conformance to quality standards and inspect materials and products for defects to ensure conformance to specifications.
Operate machinery used in the production process, assist machine operators, and start machines or equipment to begin production processes.
Clean and maintain machinery, equipment, and work areas to ensure proper functioning and safe working conditions, including washing, lubricating, or using cleaning solvents on machines and equipment.

Transfer materials and products to and from machinery and equipment using hand trucks, powered lift trucks, or other moving devices, and load and unload items from machines, conveyors, and conveyances.
Record production and operational data, such as the amount of materials processed, and maintain logs of recycling materials received or shipped to processing companies.

Rebuild, repair, or replace biofuels processing equipment components, perform routine maintenance on mechanical, electrical, or electronic equipment, and troubleshoot or repair mechanical, hydraulic, or electrical malfunctions related to equipment.
Measure, calibrate, and monitor various processes, including measuring raw biofuels feedstock and assessing the quality of biofuels additives for reprocessing.
Collect and analyze samples, perform routine laboratory tests or analyses to assess biofuels quality, and record details of repairs made and parts used.
Train and supervise workers who are assembling or disassembling equipment or performing maintenance and repair tasks.
Dismantle machines for repair, inspect or test damaged machine parts, and reassemble machines after repair or maintenance work.
Prepare raw materials for processing, including measuring, mixing, and adding ingredients according to specified procedures or formulas.
Experiment with and record test results, machine error messages, and information to diagnose equipment problems and ensure operational efficiency.
Assist with the maintenance and operation of wind turbine equipment, including performing routine checks, disassembling for repairs, and ensuring equipment operates correctly.
Operate various machines and tools to fabricate or repair parts and equipment, including cutting, welding, and fitting components.
Attend to and schedule the use of recreation facilities, maintain and provide equipment to participants of sporting events or recreational pursuits.
Direct work crews installing residential or commercial solar photovoltaic or thermal systems as well as coordinate activities related to those installations.

Read and understand operating manuals, blueprints, and technical drawings; examine parts for defects such as breakage or excessive wear; clean, lubricate, and perform routine maintenance work on equipment and vehicles; repair or replace defective parts using hand tools, milling and woodworking machines, lathes, welding equipment, grinders, or saws; test mechanical products and equipment after repair or assembly to ensure proper performance and compliance with manufacturers' specifications; dismantle and reassemble machinery and equipment for inspection, testing, or repairs; monitor safety procedures and equipment to maintain a safe working environment; supervise the work of others and assess their performance, including coordinating activities of construction or extractive workers; participate in hiring processes and initiate personnel actions; provide technical assistance to installers and technicians; and conduct preventative maintenance and repairs of machinery and equipment.

Repair, repair, and fabricate equipment or machinery as needed; coordinate maintenance schedules and keep records of service; troubleshoot and diagnose faults or malfunctions to determine required repairs, including using engine diagnostic equipment; supervise solar installers and technicians to ensure compliance with safety standards; inspect construction sites to verify that safety precautions are followed and project specifications are met; plan and coordinate installations of photovoltaic solar systems; prepare project proposals, quotes, budgets, or schedules; monitor work of contractors to ensure project compliance with plans, specifications, and budgets; evaluate solar system performance; and assess site feasibility and design requirements for solar installations.
Drive trucks to haul tools and equipment, operate various forms of machinery and equipment to transport materials, and maintain inventory of supplies necessary for installations and repairs.
Train workers in construction methods, operation of equipment, safety procedures, and company policies.
Assign work to employees based on material or worker requirements of specific jobs, and direct and train other workers to change rolls, operate mill equipment, and load material.
Order or requisition materials or supplies, and maintain inventories of equipment, storing, retrieving, and assembling items as necessary.
Record information such as personnel, production, or operational data, and maintain records of attendance, sales, receipts, and repair activities.
Locate, measure, and mark site locations or placement of structures or equipment using measuring and marking equipment.
Start machine units and conveyors and observe lights and gauges on panel board to verify operational efficiency, and start machines and set controls to regulate parameters.
Clean work areas and maintain equipment storage areas to ensure that inventory is protected.
Cut, shape, and finish rough blocks of building or monumental stone according to diagrams or patterns, and drill holes and cut or carve moldings and grooves in stone.
Provide assistance to patrons entering or exiting amusement rides, boats, or ski lifts, and monitor activities to ensure adherence to rules and safety procedures.

Verify depths and dimensions of cuts or carvings to ensure adherence to specifications, blueprints, or models, and record manufacturing information such as quantities, sizes, or types of goods produced.
Inspect equipment to detect wear and damage, and perform minor repairs, adjustments, or maintenance tasks, such as oiling parts.
Design spaces to display pottery for sale, and prepare work for sale or exhibition.
Pack and ship pottery to stores or galleries for retail sale, and sell tickets and collect fees from customers.
Direct patrons to rides, seats, or attractions, and provide information about facilities, entertainment options, and rules and regulations.
Move freight, stock, or other materials to and from storage or production areas using equipment or by hand.

Conduct employee training in equipment operations, work and safety procedures, or assign training tasks to experienced workers. Direct and coordinate the activities of employees engaged in the production or processing of goods, such as inspectors, machine setters, or fabricators. Monitor employees' work levels and review work performance to improve motivation, equipment performance, and work methods. Confer with other supervisors to coordinate operations and activities within or between departments. Enforce safety rules and regulations, and provide employee safety training to prevent, detect, and correct unsafe conditions or violations of procedures and safety rules.

Inspect, test, and measure completed work using devices such as hand tools, gauges, or other indicators to verify conformance to standards or repair requirements. Examine, measure, or weigh cargo or materials to determine specific handling requirements. Conduct or arrange for worker training in safety, repair, or maintenance techniques, operational procedures, or equipment use. Investigate accidents or injuries and prepare reports of findings. Prepare, compile, and submit reports on work activities, operations, production, or work-related accidents.

Plan work assignments and equipment allocations to meet transportation or production goals. Order tooling for jobs, requisition materials and supplies such as tools, equipment, or replacement parts, and monitor tool and part inventories. Maintain operations data, such as time, production, and cost records, and prepare management reports of production results. Analyze and record personnel or operational data and write related activity reports.

Review orders, production schedules, blueprints, and shipping or receiving notices to determine work sequences and material shipping dates, types, volumes, or destinations. Manage and monitor processes to ensure adherence to production standards. Recommend or implement measures to improve worker motivation, work methods, or equipment performance.

Inspect work areas, examine tools and equipment, and provide employee safety training to prevent, detect, and correct unsafe conditions or violations of procedures and safety rules. Allocate work schedules, and monitor compliance with safety and sanitation regulations. Confer with personnel, such as management or union workers' representatives, to coordinate work activities and resolve employee grievances.

Ultra high frequency UHF radio communication systems

Plasma-enhanced chemical vapor deposition PECVD machines
Ammonium nitrate fuel oil ANFO loaders
